channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A Polymarket trader made $300,000 betting on Biden's pardons, a new analysis shows
item.description: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.
item.content:encoded: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Iran War timeline continues to shift. And, jury rules Live Nation acted as monopoly
item.description: Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.
item.content:encoded:
Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Mediators are pushing to extend the U.S.-Iran ceasefire
item.description: Pakistan said it expected to host a second round of negotiations between the U.S. and Iran to end the war, but did not say when or where the meetings would take place.
item.content:encoded:
Pakistan said it expected to host a second round of negotiations between the U.S. and Iran to end the war, but did not say when or where the meetings would take place.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: How seals' whiskers make them master underwater hunters
item.description: Their sensitive facial hair may be the harbor seals superpower for tracking fish, scientists are learning.
item.content:encoded:
Their sensitive facial hair may be the harbor seals superpower for tracking fish, scientists are learning.

(Image credit: Robin Heinrich/Marine Science Center)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Indonesia's capital of the future faces doubts in the present
item.description: Indonesia is racing to build a new capital, promising a greener, futuristic city. But many citizens aren't convinced it will live up to the vision.
item.content:encoded:
Indonesia is racing to build a new capital, promising a greener, futuristic city. But many citizens aren't convinced it will live up to the vision.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: 'Dear America': HUD workers say they're being blocked from doing their jobs
item.description: A website with anonymous employee letters accuses the Trump administration of undermining work on housing discrimination. HUD says it's restoring "sanity" to fair housing enforcement.
item.content:encoded:
A website with anonymous employee letters accuses the Trump administration of undermining work on housing discrimination. HUD says it's restoring "sanity" to fair housing enforcement.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Jet fuel prices double, leading airlines to increase baggage fees, raise fares
item.description: Airlines are facing higher costs, and one airport group in Europe has warned of the risk of a "systemic jet fuel shortage" if traffic through the Strait of Hormuz doesn't normalize by the end of this month.
item.content:encoded:
Airlines are facing higher costs, and one airport group in Europe has warned of the risk of a "systemic jet fuel shortage" if traffic through the Strait of Hormuz doesn't normalize by the end of this month.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: We watched 2 focus groups of Georgia swing voters. They're not happy with the Iran war
item.description: None of the 13 focus group participants — who all voted for President Trump in 2024 — said they would describe the military action in Iran as going well so far.
item.content:encoded:
None of the 13 focus group participants — who all voted for President Trump in 2024 — said they would describe the military action in Iran as going well so far.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: 3 things to know about naval blockades as U.S. begins patrols in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The White House says it wants to choke off Iran's oil export revenue. But experts say that blockades are often unpredictable and difficult to enforce.
item.content:encoded:
The White House says it wants to choke off Iran's oil export revenue. But experts say that blockades are often unpredictable and difficult to enforce.

(Image credit: Jesse Monford)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: President Trump's timeline for an end to the Iran war continues to shift, U.S. and Iran block the Strait of Hormuz, trapping the Gulf's oil and gas, Trump's allies defend his remarks about Pope Leo.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's timeline for an end to the Iran war continues to shift, U.S. and Iran block the Strait of Hormuz, trapping the Gulf's oil and gas, Trump's allies defend his remarks about Pope Leo.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel
